sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.11 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: An element$g$of a group$G$is said to be generalized periodic\/ if there exist$x_1, ,x_n G$such that$x_1^{-1}gx_1 x_n^{-1}gx_n= 1$. Does there exist a finitely generated torsion-free group all of whose elements are generalized periodic? { Yu.\,M.\,Gorchakov} Yes, there does (A.\,P.\,Goryushkin, Siberian Math.~J., { 14} (1973), 146--148). Another example is$G= < a,b (b^2)^{a}=b^{-2},\;\; (a^2)^{b}=a...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
